steps.title: Combine terms with a on the left
steps.content: Start with $5a = \frac{4b^2}{a} - 20a$. Add $20a$ to both sides to get $5a + 20a = \frac{4b^2}{a}$, which simplifies to $25a = \frac{4b^2}{a}$.

steps.title: Clear the denominator
steps.content: Multiply both sides by $a$ to obtain $25a^2 = 4b^2$.

steps.title: Isolate a and take the square root
steps.content:
Divide both sides by 25:
$$a^2 = \frac{4b^2}{25}$$
Take the positive square root (since $a > 0$):
$$a = \sqrt{\frac{4b^2}{25}} = \frac{2b}{5}$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
